title: palicourea coriacea
intro: Palicourea coriacea, also known as the 'Red Ironbark' or 'Red Ironwood', is a plant species that belongs to the Rubiaceae family. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Palicourea coriacea include its ability to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to exhibit antioxidant and antiparasitic activities. Palicourea coriacea is often prepared as a herbal tea or decoction, and its bark and leaves can be used to make infusions or tinctures, although its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential toxicity.
